en.name: 1,3-beta-D-glucan in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context (1→3)-β-D-glucan is a fungal cell wall polysaccharide used as a panfungal serum biomarker in immunocompromised patients to support the diagnosis of invasive fungal infection (invasive candidiasis, aspergillosis, *Pneumocystis* pneumonia) [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by immunoassay. ## Included Concepts The set lists one LOINC "Lab Test" concept (no LOINC Hierarchy available in the loaded vocabulary): - *3034360 / 42176-8 — 1,3 beta glucan [Mass/volume] in Serum (LOINC)* **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`pg/mL`** (concept 8845 — picogram per millilitre). ## Excluded Concepts The set deliberately omits: - *1001830 / 93812-6 — 1,3 beta glucan [Presence] in Serum*: qualitative, not a concentration. - *36659624 / 95072-5 — 1,3 beta glucan [Mass/volume] in Cerebral spinal fluid*: out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - *36303298 / 88923-8 — 1,3 beta glucan [Mass/volume] in Lower respiratory specimen*: out of scope. - *40765205 / 62468-4 — 1,3 beta glucan [Mass/volume] in Specimen*: under-specified specimen. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports a 1,3-beta-D-glucan value: `3034360 / 42176-8 — 1,3 beta glucan [Mass/volume] in Serum`. - **Source reports beta-glucan from CSF, bronchoalveolar lavage or other respiratory specimens**: do **not** map. Out of scope. - **Do not confuse with**: galactomannan (Aspergillus-specific serum antigen, different analyte), Cryptococcus antigen. ## References [1] Theel ES, Doern CD. β-D-glucan testing is important for diagnosis of invasive fungal infections. *J Clin Microbiol*. 2013;51(11):3478-83. Available: https://doi.org/10.1128/JCM.01737-13 [2] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 42176-8 — 1,3 beta glucan [Mass/volume] in Serum. Available: https://loinc.org/42176-8/
